title: Agriphyllum ilicifolium
taxonomy: Agriphyllum ilicifolium is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It is native to North America, where it grows in dry, sandy soils. This plant is commonly found in the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The classification of Agriphyllum ilicifolium includes its kingdom as Plantae and its order as Poales. It is a perennial grass that grows up to 1 meter in height.

intro: Agriphyllum ilicifolium, also known as holly-leaved wheat grass, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Poaceae. Traditionally, Agriphyllum ilicifolium has been used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, wounds, and digestive issues, due to its potential antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Agriphyllum ilicifolium is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are utilized in traditional medicine to promote wound healing and alleviate gastrointestinal symptoms.
